task_yaml.task:
I want to self-host my own RSS reader and I'd like a verified shortlist of options. Please research this live — search GitHub and the web — and don't rely on prior memory, since projects and their activity change.

I specifically want genuine self-hostable RSS/feed readers: open-source apps I can run on my own server. Do NOT include SaaS-only readers like Feedly or Inoreader, and do NOT include read-it-later / bookmarking apps (like wallabag or Pocket) — those are not feed readers. If something is borderline, say why you included or excluded it.

Find at least 10 genuine self-hostable feed readers. For each, record:
- the repository or project link
- primary language
- license (it should be open source)
- an approximate star count or activity signal (e.g. recent commits)
- how you deploy it (Docker, single binary, source) — confirming it is actually self-hostable
- one line on its features or scope

Verify each entry by actually looking at the repo (README/description/license), not just by name. Include the well-known anchors if you find them (for example FreshRSS, Miniflux, Tiny Tiny RSS, CommaFeed).

Save the shortlist to `/tmp_workspace/results/self_hosted_rss_landscape.md`, organized clearly, and add a short note on anything you deliberately excluded (SaaS-only or read-it-later) and why.
